page: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-32 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the N1 machine family. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 29.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of approximately 0.9 GB per vCPU. It does not include bundled local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates a clear divergence between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the weak category, and single-threaded gzip compression is poor. However, the 32 vCPUs deliver average multi-core performance in stress-ng, PassMark, and Geekbench multi-core tests. Memory bandwidth, static web serving throughput, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression also perform within the average tier.

The primary tradeoff of the n1-highcpu-32 is its low memory density and weak single-threaded execution speed, which limits its utility for memory-intensive databases or single-threaded applications. From a cost-efficiency perspective, it offers a high density of dedicated vCPUs relative to memory capacity, making it a viable option for parallelizable, compute-bound workloads that do not require large memory footprints. Appropriate workloads include multi-threaded software compilation, image processing, ray tracing, and parallelized batch processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-32 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs across 16 physical Intel Xeon cores running at 2.3 GHz, paired with 29.0 GB of memory. It lacks bundled local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks reveal weak single-core capabilities, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores in the bottom 25%. However, its multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and static web serving throughput fall into the average tier. The instance offers a high vCPU-to-RAM ratio, presenting a qualitative cost-efficiency advantage for parallelized, compute-bound tasks that do not require substantial memory. It is best suited for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, and image processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-32 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 29.0 GB RAM, offering average multi-core performance for parallelized workloads.

tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring thirty-two dedicated vCPUs and a low memory footprint for highly parallelized, compute-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical Intel Xeon cores at 2.3 GHz
29.0 GB of system memory, providing 0.9 GB of RAM per vCPU
Average multi-core performance in Geekbench and PassMark benchmarks
Weak single-core and single-threaded compression performance
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for parallelized tasks like software compilation and image processing
